channel.title: Not a Peeker of Nature
channel.description: Recent content on Not a Peeker of Nature

item.title: Skills to learn
item.description: I find there are many useful skills to learn during my research. Sometimes I cannot learn them immediately, so I record them in the post for further study. BIOS stands for 1. Basis of github 2. Routine pipelines 3. Docker and singularity To be continued …

item.title: Basis_of_computer
item.description: 1.BIOS What? BIOS stands for ‘Basic Input/Output System’. Why? Whether SVM mode (for AMD; VT-x for Intel) is enabled in BIOS can affect the functionality of wsl2.

item.title: Routine_analysis_in_bioinformatics
item.description: Why? There are two main reasons why I want to record these routine pipelines: (1) save some time if Routine analysis 1: volcano plot To be continued …

item.title: Routine_pipelines_in_bioinformatics
item.description: Why? There are lots of packages dealing with various routine analysis in bioinformatics. Many of them are R packages Routine analysis 1: volcano plot To be continued …

item.title: Draw figures for publications
item.description: Why? Usually, I use Python with ggplot style to draw plots and take screenshots of date displayed in browser. Then, I organize them into a full figure in PPT. But there are two problems that trouble me: one is the low resolution, and the other is the inconvenience when converting from one layout to another. When I read the post “Drawing Vector Graphics For Publication Figures” published by Prof. Xi Chen, I realized there are some better methods.

item.title: Issues collection about virtual environment
item.description: Why? Configuring environment is quite important, and sometimes annoying especially for a beginner. The issues can be various, so I’d like to collect them in the post. There are two main benefits for me: one is to save time if I have the same issue next time, another is to build a framework of virtual environment so that I can really understand how it works. Background information h Issues To be continued …

item.title: Nomenclature of genes and proteins
item.description: Why? When I wrote my thesis, I did not write the gene names in italics. Here is the summary I find in Zhihu. I think the summary is clear and enough for me.

item.title: About me (all text)
item.description: About me My name is Pengfei Yin (殷鹏飞). I completed my BSc at the Ocean University of China, where I was deeply attracted to how mechanisms underlying molecular biology are discovered. Since then, I have been systematically enhancing my ability to decipher biological mechanisms. In addition to experiments, numerous scientific studies demonstrate that Physics (Francis Crick) and computational analysis can also help us understand the biological phenomena. Thus, I have developed a background in molecular biology, complemented by training in mathematical modeling and core courses for Physics students (including the 3 volumes of the Feynman Lectures on Physics).

item.title: SSH service
item.description: SSH service Why? There are two main reasons for me to start SSH service on WSL: It’s convenient for me to operate my host machine in the lab. As long as there is a low-end, lightweight MacBook, we can deal with some tasks with SSH connecting to my host machine How? I tried many tutorials on the Internet, most of them don’t work. So, I want to record how to do it step by step (Reference blog).

item.title: HUGO, follow-up
item.description: Hello, HUGO! Why? Inspired by the website of Prof.XiChen, I also want to build my own website using HUGO with gokarna theme. I think it’s a good way to record my thoughts and progresses, as well as a method to present myself. Besides, it can also encourage me to write more, which I think, is quite important to reorganize various pieces of knowledge and assimilate them into my own understanding.

item.title: About me (with figure)
item.description: About me The following pic displays my motivation, research interests, skills and goals

item.title: About me (with figure)
item.description: About me My name is Pengfei Yin (殷鹏飞). I completed my BSc at the Ocean University of China, where I was deeply attracted to how mechanisms underlying molecular biology are discovered. Since then, I have been systematically enhancing my ability to decipher biological mechanisms. In addition to experiments, numerous scientific studies demonstrate that Physics (Francis Crick) and computational analysis can also help us understand the biological phenomena. Thus, I have developed a background in molecular biology, complemented by training in mathematical modeling and core courses for Physics students (including the 3 volumes of the Feynman Lectures on Physics).

item.title: Thesis project
item.description: Thesis project: Dynamic 3D chromatin architecture of active and inactive regions and gene regulation of Drosophila under heat stress if load the figures slowly, please click the link to read its pdf format Fig.1 and Fig.S1 (Experiment design and good data quality) The experiment design (Fig.1A) High reproducibility (Fig.1B,C,D and Fig.S1A,C) Typical heat shock related genes are up-regulated (Fig.1D and Fig.S1B) Fig1 FigS1 Fig.2 and Fig.S2 (The most interesting features blocks in H3K27me3 and stripes in H3K27ac are found through loop analysis) Loop analysis indicates there is something interesting(Fig.

item.title: Hello, HUGO!
item.description: Hello, HUGO! Why? Inspired by the website of Prof. Xi Chen, I also want to build my own website using HUGO with gokarna. I think it’s a good way to record my thoughts and progresses, as well as a method to present myself. Besides, it can also encourage me to write more, which I think, is quite important to reorganize various pieces of knowledge and assimilate them into my own understanding.
